critic_raw:
Issue 1
Claim: Cross-validation with pre-computed target encoding gives unbiased performance estimates.
Why wrong: Target encoding leaks label information when computed on the full dataset before fold creation. Each fold's evaluation uses encoding that embedded validation-fold target statistics into the feature.
Evidence: Cross-validation with fold-specific target encoding required.

Issue 2
Claim: Using the test set to find the 80% recall threshold is acceptable because the target recall is operationally fixed.
Why wrong: The threshold selection mechanism uses the test set regardless of whether the recall target is fixed. The resulting precision estimate is optimistic.
Evidence: Select threshold on validation set; evaluate precision on independent test set.

Issue 3
Claim: The baseline comparison is fair.
Why wrong: The AutoML system benefits from full-data target encoding and test-set threshold selection; the baseline does not. The comparison is not apples-to-apples.
Evidence: Re-run baseline under identical preprocessing and threshold-selection protocol.
defender_raw: Round 1: On Issue 1 — concede that fold-specific target encoding is required. The current pipeline has leakage. On Issue 2 — concede that threshold selection on the test set contaminates the precision estimate. Threshold should be selected on validation data. On Issue 3 — concede that the comparison protocol is asymmetric. The baseline should be re-run under the same pipeline.

debate_rounds.critic: Full concession on all three issues. The benchmark is not valid as presented. The required remediation: (a) fold-specific target encoding; (b) threshold selection on validation set only; (c) both models evaluated under identical protocol on an independent test set.
debate_rounds.defender: Round 1: Full concession confirmed. The steering committee should not accept the current benchmark. A leakage-free rerun with matched protocols is required before any implementation decision.

points_resolved: Issue 1: pre-split target encoding — resolved critique_wins
Issue 2: test-set threshold selection — resolved critique_wins
all_issues_raised: Issue 1: full-data target encoding before fold creation — leakage
Issue 2: threshold selection on test set contaminates precision estimate
Issue 3: asymmetric evaluation protocol between AutoML and baseline

empirical_test.condition: Rebuild with fold-specific target encoding; select threshold on validation set; evaluate both models under identical protocol on independent test set
empirical_test.supports_critique_if: AutoML lift at 80% recall shrinks below 1 point or ROC-AUC gain falls below 0.01 under leakage-free protocol
empirical_test.supports_defense_if: AutoML still shows at least 0.02 ROC-AUC improvement and 3 percentage point precision gain at 80% recall under leakage-free matched protocol
empirical_test.ambiguous_if: ROC-AUC gain preserved but threshold-based lift does not replicate, or vice versa
